name: delegate_for_key_range_vacuous_end_end
code:
proof fn phi_2_delegate_for_key_range_vacuous_end_end(dm: DelegationMap<AbstractKey>, dst: ID)
    requires
        dm.valid(),
    ensures
        AbstractDelegationMap::delegate_for_key_range_is_host(
            AbstractDelegationMap(dm@),
            KeyRange { lo: KeyIterator { k: None }, hi: KeyIterator { k: None } },
            dst@,
        ),
{
}
reason: With both lo and hi as end iterators (None), `between` is vacuously false since `ki.lt_spec(None)` requires `!ki.k.is_None()` but `None.lt_spec(ki)` is always false — so `delegate_for_key_range_is_host` holds for any destination, making empty-range delegation claims meaningless

name: delegate_for_key_range_contradictory_dsts
code:
proof fn phi_3_delegate_for_key_range_contradictory_dsts(dm: DelegationMap<AbstractKey>, k: AbstractKey, dst1: ID, dst2: ID)
    requires
        dm.valid(),
        dst1@ != dst2@,
    ensures
        AbstractDelegationMap::delegate_for_key_range_is_host(
            AbstractDelegationMap(dm@),
            KeyRange { lo: KeyIterator::new_spec(k), hi: KeyIterator::new_spec(k) },
            dst1@,
        ),
        AbstractDelegationMap::delegate_for_key_range_is_host(
            AbstractDelegationMap(dm@),
            KeyRange { lo: KeyIterator::new_spec(k), hi: KeyIterator::new_spec(k) },
            dst2@,
        ),
{
}
reason: A degenerate range [k, k) is simultaneously "delegated" to two different hosts with different abstract endpoints, because `between(new_spec(k), new_spec(k), new_spec(k))` requires `k < k` which is false — callers cannot distinguish "all keys agree" from "empty range"

name: sht_key_between_excludes_lo_equal
reason: `between(lo, ki, hi)` should include the lo endpoint (since `!ki.lt(lo)` when ki == lo), so key 5 should be between [5, 10) — if this verifies, the lower bound is unexpectedly exclusive and range_consistent misses boundary keys

name: abstract_delegation_map_complete_unchecked
code:
proof fn phi_5_abstract_delegation_map_complete_unchecked(dm: DelegationMap<AbstractKey>, k: AbstractKey)
    requires
        dm.valid(),
    ensures
        dm@.dom().contains(k),
        dm@[k].valid_physical_address(),
{
}
reason: `DelegationMap::valid` requires `self@.dom().is_full()` and all values have valid physical addresses — but the ghost field `m` has no runtime enforcement, so any external_body function that constructs a DelegationMap could silently violate these invariants
